title: Length of Longest Increasing Subsequence
description:
Given Compute the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in nums. Implement the patience-sorting O(n log n) method for typing practice, though a quadratic DP exists.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 2500
- -10^4 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4
explanation:
Intuition
Maintain an array tails where tails[k] is the smallest possible tail value of an increasing subsequence of length k+1. For each x, binary search its position and replace or append.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize empty tails
2. Main Algorithm: For each x in nums, binary search first ≥ x in tails and set tails[pos]=x else append
3. Return Result: Length of tails is LIS length

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n log n)
- Space Complexity: O(n)

Key Insights
- tails is not an actual subsequence but encodes lengths
- Strictly increasing means lower_bound on ≥ x

Common Pitfalls
- Using > instead of ≥ breaks duplicates handling
- Returning sum or content of tails is incorrect

testCases.explanation: When length_of_lis(nums=[0,1,0,3,2,3]), the expected result is 4
When length_of_lis(nums=[7,7,7,7]), the expected result is 1
